title: Tontelea decussata
intro: Tontelea decussata, also known as the "Tontelea vine," is a woody climber plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a natural remedy for respiratory health. Bioactive constituents of Tontelea decussata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tontelea decussata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and other ailments.
taxonomy: Tontelea decussata belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family includes over 2,000 species of flowering plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tontelea decussata is native to Central and South America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Tontelea, a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their unique, often complex flowers. The specific classification of Tontelea decussata reflects its evolutionary history and distinct morphological characteristics within its family and genus.
